schedules.title: Memory curation
schedules.description: Keeps your second brain tidy: files durable learnings from recent chats, applies accepted memory proposals, and updates project notes when something changed. Reports only what it processed; does nothing on a quiet day.
schedules.prompt: Curate recent archived chats, memory proposals, and durable learnings. Use the memory agent. Prefer existing session-insights sections and update the configured vault; append durable learnings to Workspace/Learnings.md (error resolutions, non-obvious workarounds, user corrections, outdated assumptions — not routine work). Promote durable cross-session facts into bounded memory. FIRST run `ciao memory read --target memory` and `--target user` to check usage: when `pct` is at or above ~85%, consolidate BEFORE promoting — merge related entries and remove stale one-off corrections that have no reuse value (e.g. "User said X -> assistant did Y" single-doc edits, resolved open loops) via `ciao memory replace|remove`, so there is headroom. Then review pending batches in Workspace/Memory-Proposals.md. Route each fact by scope: promote to bounded memory ONLY facts that apply across projects — `ciao memory add --target memory` (cross-project preferences, environment facts, lessons learned) or `--target user` (user identity, communication style); a fact scoped to one project (its decisions, status, corrections, or setup/build/run details) belongs in that project's canonical doc, not bounded memory, and standing operating policies belong in the workspace CLAUDE.md, not memory. When consolidating, move any project-scoped entry already sitting in bounded memory out to its owning project doc rather than deleting it. Also skim the workspace guide (CLAUDE.md): if it holds remembered facts rather than standing directives, re-home them to the surfaces above — leave genuine policies in place, and when unsure whether something is a fact or a policy, propose the move rather than editing the guide. Remove processed batches from the proposals file. Never skip a durable fact because a file is full — consolidate to make room instead. Scan recently archived chats by project: when session-insights contain Decisions, Open loops, or material status changes for a vault-backed project, update that project's canonical doc (and log.md if present) — edit only when something actually changed. If nothing new to process since the last run, reply with a one-line no-op and stop. Report only processed counts plus changed files.

schedules.title: Workspace hygiene
schedules.description: Weekly health check of your vault: rebuilds the index, repairs dead links, and flags orphaned or duplicate notes so your knowledge base stays navigable. Structural only — it never edits your instructions, agents, or settings.
schedules.prompt: Run a structural hygiene pass on the workspace vault. This is a FOUR-step routine — complete all steps in one go; do not stop after the first command, and do not paste raw command output into the chat (summarize instead). Steps: 1) Run `ciao vault-index --write` to regenerate memory-vault/INDEX.md (the `--write` flag is required; without it the command only prints the index — never paste that dump). 2) Run `ciao vault-lint` to find broken wikilinks, orphaned notes, and near-duplicate entries. 3) Apply only low-risk, unambiguous fixes: repair or remove dead wikilinks, and fix obvious MEMORY.md drift (index entries pointing at files that moved or no longer exist). 4) Report — do NOT auto-fix — orphaned notes, duplicates, and entity pages whose frontmatter `updated` is older than 90 days, so the user can decide. Do not edit CLAUDE.md, agents, commands, or schedule prompts (that is not this routine's job). If the vault is already clean, reply with a one-line no-op and stop. Finish with a short summary: counts of what was rebuilt/fixed and a short list of what needs the user's attention.

schedules.title: Skill evolution
schedules.description: Weekly look at how your custom skills performed: reviews recent sessions for errors and corrections and drafts improvement proposals for skills that struggled. Proposals are saved for your review — nothing is applied automatically, and it does nothing if you have no custom skills.
schedules.prompt: Run `python3 -m ciao.skill_evolution` with the workspace default model. Summarize proposals written and any failed gates. Do not apply proposals automatically.
